tip: The Chinese word for 'hi' is the friendliest opener. Mandarin has tones — 你好 is nǐ-hǎo (3rd + 3rd, but the first shifts to 2nd tone in speech).

words.notes: The universal greeting. Two 3rd-tones: first becomes 2nd in speech.
words.image_prompt: two cheerful friends waving hello to each other in a sunny park

tip: Politeness in Chinese often comes from word choice. 您 (nín) is the respectful 'you'; 您好 is more formal than 你好.

words.notes: 您 is the respectful 'you' — use with elders, customers, bosses.
words.image_prompt: a person bowing politely at an office entrance

words.notes: Literally 'again-see'. 拜拜 (bài bài) is casual.
words.image_prompt: two colleagues waving goodbye at a train station

tip: More Chinese vocabulary is coming soon for this day. Keep practicing what you've learned!

words.notes: 是 means 'to be' and also confirms 'yes'.
words.image_prompt: a smiling person giving a thumbs up with a checkmark

words.notes: 不 negates verbs. Tone changes to bú before a 4th tone.
words.image_prompt: a person gently shaking their head

tip: More Chinese vocabulary is coming soon for this day. Keep practicing what you've learned!

words.notes: The essential courtesy word. Reply: 不客气.
words.image_prompt: two hands passing a small gift wrapped in ribbon

words.notes: Put 请 before a verb to make a polite request.
words.image_prompt: a waiter politely gesturing to a seat

tip: More Chinese vocabulary is coming soon for this day. Keep practicing what you've learned!

words.notes: Sincere apology. Reply: 没关系 (it's okay).
words.image_prompt: a person bowing slightly in apology

words.notes: Softer than 对不起; used to get attention or for minor sorries.
words.image_prompt: a person politely tapping someone's shoulder

tip: More Chinese vocabulary is coming soon for this day. Keep practicing what you've learned!

words.notes: 吗 turns a statement into a yes/no question.
words.image_prompt: two friends meeting on a bridge asking how they are

words.notes: 很 (very) is needed before adjectives even for neutral meaning.
words.image_prompt: a smiling sun over a peaceful flower garden

tip: More Chinese vocabulary is coming soon for this day. Keep practicing what you've learned!

words.notes: No change for subject/object. 我 is always 'I' or 'me'.
words.image_prompt: a person pointing to themselves with a friendly smile

words.notes: Casual 'you'. Formal: 您.
words.image_prompt: a person pointing forward warmly at the viewer

words.notes: Respectful 'you'. 您贵姓 is a polite way to ask a name.
words.image_prompt: two people greeting each other respectfully

tip: More Chinese vocabulary is coming soon for this day. Keep practicing what you've learned!

words.notes: 他/她/它 all sound 'tā' — only writing differs!
words.image_prompt: a young man waving from across a street

words.notes: Sounds identical to 他 (he). Context/writing distinguishes.
words.image_prompt: a kind woman holding an open book

words.notes: 们 makes pronouns plural: 我→我们, 你→你们.
words.image_prompt: four friends linking arms and laughing

words.notes: 们 marks plural people.
words.image_prompt: a small group of students walking with backpacks

tip: More Chinese vocabulary is coming soon for this day. Keep practicing what you've learned!

words.notes: 的 marks possession: 我的 = my, 你的 = your.
words.image_prompt: a hand holding a small name tag

words.notes: Add 的 to any pronoun/noun for possession.
words.image_prompt: two hands exchanging a bag

tip: More Chinese vocabulary is coming soon for this day. Keep practicing what you've learned!

words.notes: Repeated syllable, soft 2nd. 母亲 is formal.
words.image_prompt: a loving mother hugging her child in a sunny kitchen

words.notes: Repeated syllable. 父亲 is formal.
words.image_prompt: a father reading a bedtime story to his child

tip: More Chinese vocabulary is coming soon for this day. Keep practicing what you've learned!

words.notes: Means both 'family' and 'home'. Counter for family: 口.
words.image_prompt: a three-generation family around a dinner table

words.notes: Chinese distinguishes older/younger siblings by word.
words.image_prompt: two brothers playing together

words.notes: 弟弟=younger bro, 妹妹=younger sis.
words.image_prompt: two siblings sharing an umbrella in gentle rain

tip: More Chinese vocabulary is coming soon for this day. Keep practicing what you've learned!

words.notes: Paternal grandma. Maternal: 外婆/姥姥.
words.image_prompt: a kind grandmother cooking in a cozy kitchen

words.notes: Paternal grandpa. Maternal: 外公/姥爷.
words.image_prompt: a wise grandfather with a gentle smile reading

tip: More Chinese vocabulary is coming soon for this day. Keep practicing what you've learned!

words.notes: 好朋友 = good friend. Gender-neutral.
words.image_prompt: two friends sharing a meal at a casual cafe

words.notes: Also means 'people'. One of the first characters learned.
words.image_prompt: a friendly person smiling warmly

tip: More Chinese vocabulary is coming soon for this day. Keep practicing what you've learned!

words.notes: Same character as 'family' — home and family are one concept.
words.image_prompt: a cozy house with warm light

words.notes: 房=building, 间=counter/space for rooms.
words.image_prompt: a sunny tidy bedroom with a small bookshelf

tip: More Chinese vocabulary is coming soon for this day. Keep practicing what you've learned!

words.sentence_en: Mom is cooking in the kitchen.
words.notes: 厨=cooking, 房=room.
words.image_prompt: a warm kitchen with a steaming wok

words.notes: Literally 'wash-hands-room'. Essential travel phrase!
words.image_prompt: a clean simple bathroom door with a sign

tip: More Chinese vocabulary is coming soon for this day. Keep practicing what you've learned!

words.notes: Simple character that looks like a door (門 traditional).
words.image_prompt: an old wooden door slightly ajar

words.notes: 窗 = window; 户 softens to neutral tone.
words.image_prompt: an open window with curtains, garden outside

tip: More Chinese vocabulary is coming soon for this day. Keep practicing what you've learned!

words.sentence_en: The book is on the table.
words.notes: 子 is a common noun suffix (neutral tone).
words.image_prompt: a wooden desk with a vase of flowers

words.notes: Counter for chairs: 把 (yì bǎ yǐzi).
words.image_prompt: a comfortable wooden chair by a sunny window

tip: More Chinese vocabulary is coming soon for this day. Keep practicing what you've learned!

words.notes: Counter for books: 本 (yì běn shū).
words.image_prompt: an open antique book with golden pages

words.notes: Counter for pens: 支 (yì zhī bǐ).
words.image_prompt: a beautiful blue fountain pen on a letter

tip: More Chinese vocabulary is coming soon for this day. Keep practicing what you've learned!

words.sentence_en: My phone is out of battery.
words.notes: Literally 'hand-machine'.
words.image_prompt: a modern smartphone on a wooden table with tea

words.notes: Literally 'electric-brain' — a lovely word!
words.image_prompt: a laptop open on a cozy desk with a mug

tip: More Chinese vocabulary is coming soon for this day. Keep practicing what you've learned!

words.sentence_en: Please give me a glass of water.
words.notes: Essential. Counter: 杯 (cup) for a glass of water.
words.image_prompt: a clear glass of water with droplets

words.notes: 面=flour, 包=bun/wrap.
words.image_prompt: a fresh loaf of bread sliced on a board

tip: China is the birthplace of tea (茶 chá). Tea culture runs deep — green, oolong, pu'er. The word 茶 appears in many languages because of China.

words.sentence_en: I like to drink green tea.
words.notes: China is tea's homeland. 绿茶=green, 红茶=black tea.
words.image_prompt: a warm cup of green tea with steam

words.sentence_en: What do you want to drink?
words.notes: Essential verb, pairs with all drinks.
words.image_prompt: a person enjoying a hot drink by a window

tip: More Chinese vocabulary is coming soon for this day. Keep practicing what you've learned!

words.sentence_en: I want a cup of coffee.
words.notes: Loanword (sounds like 'coffee').
words.image_prompt: a steaming cup of coffee on a cafe table

words.notes: 牛=cow, 奶=milk → cow's milk.
words.image_prompt: a glass bottle of fresh milk in morning light

tip: More Chinese vocabulary is coming soon for this day. Keep practicing what you've learned!

words.notes: Counter for fruit: 个. 苹 also in 苹果手机 (iPhone).
words.image_prompt: a glossy red apple with a green leaf

words.notes: 香=fragrant, 蕉=banana plant.
words.image_prompt: a bunch of ripe yellow bananas

tip: More Chinese vocabulary is coming soon for this day. Keep practicing what you've learned!

words.notes: 猪肉=pork, 牛肉=beef, 鸡肉=chicken.
words.image_prompt: a plate of cooked meat with vegetables

words.notes: Counter for fish: 条. Sounds like 余 (surplus) — lucky!
words.image_prompt: a fresh fish on ice with lemon and herbs

tip: Rice (米饭) is the staple of southern Chinese meals, and soup (汤) often accompanies them. A bowl of rice is 一碗饭.

words.notes: Note: tasty drinks use 好喝 (good-drink), food uses 好吃.
words.image_prompt: a steaming bowl of clear soup

words.sentence_en: I want a bowl of rice.
words.notes: 米=raw rice, 饭=cooked meal. Counter: 碗 (bowl).
words.image_prompt: a bowl of fluffy white steamed rice

tip: More Chinese vocabulary is coming soon for this day. Keep practicing what you've learned!

words.notes: 蛋=egg, 糕=cake/pastry.
words.image_prompt: a slice of layered cake with berries

words.notes: Means both 'sugar' and 'candy'.
words.image_prompt: a colorful pile of wrapped candies

tip: 是 (shì) is the closest to 'to be', but Chinese skips it before adjectives — say 我很高 (I'm tall), not 我是高.

words.notes: Use 是 to link nouns. NOT used before adjectives.
words.image_prompt: a person looking thoughtfully in a mirror

tip: More Chinese vocabulary is coming soon for this day. Keep practicing what you've learned!

words.notes: 住在 + place. 在 marks location.
words.image_prompt: a person opening curtains to a sunny morning

words.notes: Also 'there is/are'. Negative: 没有.
words.image_prompt: a person happily holding a small kitten

tip: More Chinese vocabulary is coming soon for this day. Keep practicing what you've learned!

words.notes: 去 + place. Simple and very common.
words.image_prompt: a person walking down a quiet street

words.notes: Opposite of 去. 来 = toward speaker.
words.image_prompt: a person warmly welcomed at a doorway

tip: More Chinese vocabulary is coming soon for this day. Keep practicing what you've learned!

words.notes: Versatile: 看书 (read), 看电视 (watch TV).
words.image_prompt: a person curled up reading in a cozy chair

words.notes: 写字 = write characters.
words.image_prompt: a hand writing characters with a brush pen

tip: More Chinese vocabulary is coming soon for this day. Keep practicing what you've learned!

words.notes: 说话 = to talk. 会 = can (learned skill).
words.image_prompt: two people having an animated conversation

words.notes: 听懂 = understand by hearing; 看懂 = by reading.
words.image_prompt: a person having a lightbulb moment

tip: More Chinese vocabulary is coming soon for this day. Keep practicing what you've learned!

words.notes: 要 + verb/noun. Also means 'will' or 'need'.
words.image_prompt: a child looking longingly at a cake in a window

words.notes: 会 = learned ability. 能 = physical possibility.
words.image_prompt: a person extending a helping hand up a hill

tip: More Chinese vocabulary is coming soon for this day. Keep practicing what you've learned!

words.notes: 知道 for facts. 认识 for knowing people.
words.image_prompt: a person studying with a glowing lightbulb

words.sentence_en: I miss you / I think of you.
words.notes: 想 + verb = want to; 想 + person = miss them.
words.image_prompt: a person looking dreamily out a window

tip: More Chinese vocabulary is coming soon for this day. Keep practicing what you've learned!

words.notes: Also means 'okay'. Extremely common.
words.image_prompt: a thumbs up with warm golden light

words.notes: Also means broken: 坏了 = broke down.
words.image_prompt: dark clouds and rain over a small house

tip: More Chinese vocabulary is coming soon for this day. Keep practicing what you've learned!

words.notes: One of the simplest characters — looks like a big person!
words.image_prompt: a small person looking up at a huge oak tree

words.notes: Opposite of 大. Counter for cats: 只.
words.image_prompt: a tiny kitten sleeping in a teacup

tip: More Chinese vocabulary is coming soon for this day. Keep practicing what you've learned!

words.notes: 新 + noun directly: 新车 (new car).
words.image_prompt: a shiny new gift box with a ribbon

words.notes: 旧 for objects. For age of people: 老.
words.image_prompt: a charming old house with weathered shutters

tip: More Chinese vocabulary is coming soon for this day. Keep practicing what you've learned!

words.notes: 漂亮 for visual beauty. 美 is more elegant.
words.image_prompt: a stunning sunset over a peaceful lake

words.notes: Literally 'has meaning'. Opposite: 没意思.
words.image_prompt: a curious child examining an insect with a magnifying glass

tip: More Chinese vocabulary is coming soon for this day. Keep practicing what you've learned!

words.notes: 白=white/bright, 天=day/sky.
words.image_prompt: a bright sun over rolling green hills

words.notes: 晚上好 = good evening.
words.image_prompt: a starry night sky over a quiet village

tip: More Chinese vocabulary is coming soon for this day. Keep practicing what you've learned!

words.notes: 早上好 = good morning. 早 alone = casual 'morning!'.
words.image_prompt: morning sunlight on a breakfast table

words.notes: 上午=morning, 中午=noon, 下午=afternoon.
words.image_prompt: warm afternoon light over a city street

tip: More Chinese vocabulary is coming soon for this day. Keep practicing what you've learned!

words.notes: 天 = day. Very high-frequency.
words.image_prompt: a calendar with today circled, sunshine around

words.notes: 明 = bright/next.
words.image_prompt: a sunrise over mountains, a new day

words.notes: 了 marks completed action.
words.image_prompt: an old photograph fading into memory

tip: More Chinese vocabulary is coming soon for this day. Keep practicing what you've learned!

words.notes: 星期 + number! 一=1 → Monday. So logical!
words.image_prompt: a busy Monday morning office with coffee

words.notes: 星期二 = week-day-two. Just count up!
words.image_prompt: a notebook with Tuesday plans and a mug

tip: More Chinese vocabulary is coming soon for this day. Keep practicing what you've learned!

words.notes: Week-day-three. The pattern continues.
words.image_prompt: a calm midweek coffee break scene

words.notes: Week-day-four.
words.image_prompt: a friendly meeting around a table

words.notes: Week-day-five. TGIF!
words.image_prompt: friends celebrating at the end of the work week

tip: More Chinese vocabulary is coming soon for this day. Keep practicing what you've learned!

words.sentence_en: I go to the park on Saturday.
words.notes: Week-day-six.
words.image_prompt: a relaxed Saturday morning reading in pajamas

words.notes: Exception! Sunday is 星期天 (or 星期日), not 星期七.
words.image_prompt: a peaceful Sunday family brunch

tip: More Chinese vocabulary is coming soon for this day. Keep practicing what you've learned!

words.notes: Also pronounced 'shuí'. Both are correct.
words.image_prompt: a silhouette behind frosted glass, mysterious

words.notes: The most useful question word.
words.image_prompt: a question mark over a wrapped present

tip: More Chinese vocabulary is coming soon for this day. Keep practicing what you've learned!

words.notes: Also 哪儿 (nǎr) in northern speech.
words.image_prompt: a person looking at a map with a magnifying glass

words.notes: Literally 'what time'.
words.image_prompt: a clock face with question marks

tip: More Chinese vocabulary is coming soon for this day. Keep practicing what you've learned!

words.notes: Literally 'for what'. Answer with 因为 (because).
words.image_prompt: a curious child surrounded by question marks

words.sentence_en: How do I get to the station?
words.notes: 怎么 + verb = how to do something.
words.image_prompt: two travelers figuring out directions together

tip: Chinese numbers are beautifully logical and the foundation of dates, prices, and counting. 一二三 are written as simple strokes.

words.notes: Tone changes depending on what follows. Written as one stroke.
words.image_prompt: a single lit candle on a table

words.notes: 二 for counting; 两 (liǎng) before counters: 两个.
words.image_prompt: two coffee cups with steam

words.notes: Three strokes = three. Beautifully simple.
words.image_prompt: three colorful balloons against a blue sky

tip: More Chinese vocabulary is coming soon for this day. Keep practicing what you've learned!

words.notes: Sounds like 死 (death) — considered unlucky in China!
words.image_prompt: four seasons illustrated in a square

words.notes: One hand = five.
words.image_prompt: a hand showing five fingers

words.notes: 六 sounds like 流 (flow) — considered lucky!
words.image_prompt: six honey bees around a flower

tip: More Chinese vocabulary is coming soon for this day. Keep practicing what you've learned!

words.notes: 七 is generally neutral/lucky.
words.image_prompt: seven colorful umbrellas in a row

words.notes: 八 sounds like 发 (prosper) — the luckiest number!
words.image_prompt: an infinity symbol made of golden light

words.notes: 九 sounds like 久 (long-lasting) — lucky for weddings.
words.image_prompt: a baseball with number 9

words.notes: Written as a cross (十). Base of the number system.
words.image_prompt: ten gold coins stacked neatly

tip: China (中国, 'Middle Kingdom') and its capital Beijing (北京, 'Northern Capital') are essential words. The characters tell their own stories.

words.notes: 中=middle, 国=country → 'Middle Kingdom'.
words.image_prompt: a beautiful view of the Great Wall winding over green hills

words.sentence_en: I want to go to Beijing.
words.notes: 北=north, 京=capital → 'Northern Capital'.
words.image_prompt: the Forbidden City with traditional red walls and golden roofs
